identity.name: Zenith Rhal
identity.title: Githyanki Wizard · Occultist · Level 3
identity.epithet: Haunted by dark whispers but unable to relinquish their power
identity.footer: Curse of Strahd · Session Notes · 1489 DR

portrait.alt: Portrait of Zenith Rhal holding a lantern in Barovia

relationshipNodes.label: Zenith
relationshipNodes.tooltip: A desperate scholar clawing after forbidden understanding.

relationshipNodes.label: Strahd
relationshipNodes.tooltip: The master of Ravenloft sees Zenith as a useful curiosity.

relationshipNodes.label: Rahadin
relationshipNodes.tooltip: His patience is colder than the dungeons beneath the castle.

relationshipNodes.label: Madam Eva
relationshipNodes.tooltip: Her reading named the danger but not its cost.

relationshipNodes.label: Muriel
relationshipNodes.tooltip: A cautious wereraven who offered cover when the hunt closed in.

relationshipNodes.label: Vlaakith
relationshipNodes.tooltip: The lich queen Zenith fled still casts a longer shadow than Strahd.

relationshipNodes.label: The Voices
relationshipNodes.tooltip: Something ancient stirs in amber. It knows Zenith's name and has for a very long time.

loreSections.title: Chronicle of Shadows
loreSections.body:
Zenith Rhal was born into the iron certainty of the githyanki war-host, where obedience and conquest were treated as the only forms of destiny worth respecting. Even as a child, Zenith was less interested in command than in the stray fragments of forbidden history hidden inside confiscated tomes and captured relics.

That appetite for dangerous knowledge only deepened with age. While others perfected the sword, Zenith learned to read omens in cracked crystal, whispered with spirits that should have remained silent, and became convinced that power was never truly evil, only costly.

The Mists of Barovia answered that arrogance with enthusiasm. Ravenloft offered visions, amber whispers, and the kind of occult leverage Zenith had chased for years. Now every revelation comes attached to a debt, and every debt drags another thread tight around the throat.

loreSections.title: Current Disposition
loreSections.body: Zenith presents as composed and analytical, but that calm is performance more than truth. Sleep is scarce. Candlelight is preferred to darkness. There is a growing certainty that whatever began whispering in the castle dungeons still has not finished speaking.

secretBlocks.title: What He Knows
secretBlocks.body: Zenith understands that Strahd studies people the way a wizard studies a spell: patiently, from every angle, until every weakness is catalogued. He also suspects the Amber Temple is not merely a ruin but a key.

secretBlocks.title: What He Does Not Know
secretBlocks.body: Zenith does not yet realize that some of his most vivid visions are not prophecies at all. They are curated invitations from powers that have already decided he is useful. The voices in the amber have a name for him — they have simply not chosen to share it yet.

gallery.alt: Lantern light in a crypt corridor
gallery.caption: The night Zenith first realized Barovia listens.

gallery.alt: Occult notes scattered across a wooden desk
gallery.caption: Recovered notes from the castle dungeons.
A raven-guided escape through the Svalich Woods.
Dreams of amber fire and unfinished bargains.

sessionEntries.title: Capture at Ravenloft
sessionEntries.body: Separated from the party after a failed reconnaissance attempt. Zenith survived interrogation by trading fragments of truth for time, then escaped the lower halls with Muriel's help and one new voice lodged firmly in the back of his mind.

sessionEntries.title: The Tser Pool Reading
sessionEntries.body: Madam Eva laid out the cards at the Tser Pool encampment. Three of the five revealed something Zenith was not meant to see — a tower, an amber sarcophagus, and a figure that looked uncomfortably familiar. He has not shared the full reading with the others.

sessionEntries.title: Arrival in Vallaki
sessionEntries.body: The party reached Vallaki under the banner of a festival nobody wanted. Zenith spent most of the session in the Blue Water Inn's upper room transcribing everything he could remember about the castle's lower levels. The innkeeper's wife gave him a look that suggested she knew more than she was letting on.
